pr.title: !00% match HSD_TExpSetReg, cleanup of IsThrough functions
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Matched HSD_TExpSetReg in sysdolphin baselib texp, taking it from 99.79% to 100.00% according to the decomp.dev GALE01 report, while also cleaning up IsThroughColor and IsThroughAlpha by moving them to texp.h as simple static inline boolean expressions. The main texp.c change rewrote HSD_TExpSetReg around a direct HSD_TECnst view, clearer GXColor reg storage, a changed bitmask, typed value pointers, scoped switch cases, and HSD_ASSERT.
what_changed: Removed the old texp.c-local static inline IsThroughColor and IsThroughAlpha implementations that used staged temporary bools.
Added IsThroughColor and IsThroughAlpha to texp.h as concise static inline return expressions checking GX_TEV_ADD, HSD_TE_0 inputs, zero bias, and zero scale for color or alpha TEV state.
Reworked HSD_TExpSetReg to use GXColor reg[8], a changed bitmask, and HSD_TECnst* clist instead of many decompiler-style temporaries such as var_r4, var_r5, temp_r3_2, temp_r4_3, and sp18/sp14.
Changed constant-list handling from nested clist->cnst field access on HSD_TExp* to direct HSD_TECnst field access such as clist->reg, clist->comp, clist->ctype, clist->val, and clist->idx.
Replaced the explicit __assert call with HSD_ASSERT(0x591, clist->type == HSD_TE_CNST).
Kept the register update behavior organized around changed bits, GXPixModeSync, GXSetTevKColor for registers 0 through 3, GXSetTevColor for registers 4 through 6, and HSD_StateInvalidate(0x10).
smart_moves: The match was achieved by improving type knowledge rather than adding opaque matching hacks: using HSD_TECnst* exposes the fields the function is actually manipulating and removes many artificial temporaries.
Scoped case blocks allowed the code to introduce typed local pointers for U8, U16, U32, F32, and default F64 paths without fighting C declaration rules or reusing misleading temp names.
Renaming reg_bits to changed better documents the bitmask's role as the set of TEV color registers that need to be uploaded.
The IsThrough cleanup preserves the same checks while making the intended predicate obvious and reusable from the header.
decomp_lessons: When a function walks an embedded substructure, trying the embedded struct pointer type can collapse many clist->subfield accesses and may be the key to a remaining near-match.
For conversion-heavy code, replace decompiler temporaries with typed pointer locals inside switch cases; this can improve both readability and matching while retaining the same data conversions.
Do not overcomplicate simple inline predicates: chained boolean expressions can replace staged bool variables when the compiler output permits it.
Prefer project assertion macros such as HSD_ASSERT when the surrounding codebase uses them and they match the generated assert site.
A nearly matched function may still need structural cleanup rather than small expression tweaks; this PR turned a 99.79% HSD_TExpSetReg into a 100.00% match.
naming_conventions: Use descriptive bitmask names such as changed instead of reg_bits when the mask tracks modified state.
Use reg for a local GXColor register array when it models TEV register contents.
Use col for a temporary GXColor copied from a value pointer.
Use ptr for short-lived typed value pointers inside conversion switch cases.
Keep predicate names in the IsThroughColor and IsThroughAlpha style for TEV pass-through checks.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use direct HSD_TECnst field access to avoid nested HSD_TExp->cnst access patterns when the compiled code appears to operate on the constant node payload.
Introduce local typed pointer variables per switch case for u16, u32, f32, and f64 input paths to shape loads and conversions.
Inline clamp expressions were used in place of the helper clamp_color in HSD_TExpSetReg's rewritten paths, preserving explicit ternary clamp behavior around 0 and 0xFF.
The final decomp.dev report recorded one new match: main/sysdolphin/baselib/texp HSD_TExpSetReg, 99.79% to 100.00%, with +3 bytes for that function and +1312 matched bytes overall.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 matched code at 47.24%, up +0.03% and +1312 bytes, with HSD_TExpSetReg becoming a 100.00% match.
follow_up_queries: HSD_TExpSetReg HSD_TECnst clist changed GXColor reg
IsThroughColor IsThroughAlpha static inline texp.h GX_TEV_ADD HSD_TE_0
HSD_TE_U8 HSD_TE_U16 HSD_TE_U32 HSD_TE_F32 clamp color texp
